title: PostgreSQL Full-Text Search, Ranking, Language, and Abuse Budgets · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Gin Plan Evidence
summary: Search concatenates SQL, indexes raw HTML, assumes one language, ranking is unexplained, snippets render unsafe markup, and broad queries consume unbounded work. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go gin plan evidence increment in bounded explainable entry search. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Pair GIN and supporting indexes with representative EXPLAIN evidence, data scale, selectivity, write cost, and regression thresholds.
Convert bounded user input through an explicit plainto, websearch, or structured tsquery policy and reject pathological or empty requests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Gin Plan Evidence · Theory Feed Go Gin Plan Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 8298 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove admitted full-text queries, language policy, GIN plans, deterministic ranking, and safe snippets; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
